title: Tissue Preparation for Spatial Metabolomics
author: Guanshi Zhang
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d7gf9jtn
materials: 15T Fourier Transform Ion Cyclotron Resonance (FTICR) Mass Spectrometer

DAN is used at 4.4 mg/mL in 9 mL of 1:1 Ethanol:Water (Add 500 µL of 1M HCl in 4 mL milli'Q' water and 4.5 mL of ethanol); 90 °C ; 3 mm/track spacing; 16 cycles; 1250 mm/min spraying velocity; 0.05 mL/min matrix flow 15-Diaminonaphthalene (DAN 99%) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) (CAS: 2243-62-

Negative ion mode NEDC is used at 7 mg/mL in 7:3 Methanol:Water; 70 °C ; 3 mm/track spacing; 8 passes; 1200 mm/min spraying velocity; 0.12 mL/min matrix flow NEDC (N-(1-naphthyl) ethylenediamine hydrochloride) Contributed by users DAN is used at 4.4 mg/mL in 9 mL of 1:1 Ethanol:Water (Add 500 µL of 1M HCl in 4 mL milli'Q' water and 4.5 mL of ethanol); 90 °C ; 3 mm/track spacing; 16 cycles; 1250 mm/min spraying velocity; 0.05 mL/min matrix flow. 15-Diaminonaphthalene (DAN 99%) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) (CAS: 2243-62-
Power OFF TM-Sprayer. Power OFF solvent pump.

500 µL of 2:1 Chl:MeOH and cap (no septa) is stored at -70 °C until ready for analysis. Requires extra solution volume for safe storage. Chloroform (HPLC grade) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) (CAS: 67-66-3) Methanol (HPLC grade) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) (CAS: 67-56-1)

Prior to LC analysis, dry sample in speed vac and reconsititute in 100 µL of 95:5 MeOH:Chl. Methanol (HPLC grade) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) (CAS: 67-56-1) Chloroform (HPLC grade) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) (CAS: 67-66-3)

steps.title: Remove fresh frozen (liquid nitrogen) kidney...
steps.instruction: Remove fresh frozen (liquid nitrogen) kidney sample stored at -80 °C and place in cryostat set at -15 °C

steps.title: Mount the sample on chuck with...
steps.instruction: Mount the sample on chuck with minimal amount of water (one droplet) and make 7 µm sections, while keeping chuck temperature at -15 °C and blade temperature at -20 °C . Tissue-Tek Accu-Edge Disposable Microtome Blades Sakura Finetek Catalog #4689

steps.title: Thaw-mount in sequential order as outlined in Figure 1
steps.instruction: Thaw-mount in sequential order as outlined in Figure 1. Dry the sections immediately after sectioning in the cryostat chamber. Each slide will be marked with a number via a scribe. Figure 1. Sandwich model of human kidney biopsy tissue Conductive ITO Coating Glass Slides for MALDI Imaging Bruker Catalog #8237001 Superfrost Plus Microscope Slides Fischer Scientific Catalog #12-550-15 Pocket Scriber Carbide w/Magnet End Contributed by users Catalog #Westward #4KY32

steps.title: Tissues will be cut to generate...
steps.instruction: Tissues will be cut to generate adjacent slides that are alternatively subjected to histological staining or MALDI-MSI. This sandwich like assignment allows proper orientation and localization of each MALDI-MSI ion image to its histological counterpart.

steps.title: ITO slides with mounted coss-sections are...
steps.instruction: ITO slides with mounted coss-sections are then transferred directly from the cryostat to either the vacuum desiccator OR zipper bag. 4 x 6 Zipper Bag Contributed by users

steps.title: For imaging later
steps.instruction: For imaging later: Place ITO slides in a zipper bag to keep vacuum inside of the bag for storing in the -80 °C freezer (sectioned samples can be stored in the freezer for months but should be analyzed as soon as possible). When removing the bag from the freezer, keep it close for ~5 minutes to reach RT before opening it.

steps.title: Regular glass slides are stored at -80 °C untiil staining
steps.instruction: Regular glass slides are stored at -80 °C untiil staining

steps.title: All slides (regular and ITO) stored...
steps.instruction: All slides (regular and ITO) stored at -80 °C are placed in the vacuum desiccator to defrost

steps.title: For the 15T Fourier Transform Ion...
steps.instruction: For the 15T Fourier Transform Ion Cyclotron Resonance (FTICR) Mass Spectrometry, MALDI imaging slides are mounted in the Bruker MTP Slide Adapter II and scanned with a flatbed scanner with at least 3200 dpi resolution (output JPEG, TIFF, or PNG). This is performed so that the optical image can be co-registered with the imaging experiment run. Visible fiducials (e.g., "X" marks) are previously placed onto MTP Slide Adapter to use as teaching points to register the optical and MS images. MTP Slide-Adapter II Bruker Catalog #8235380 Flatbed Scanner Contributed by users

steps.title: Visible fiducials (e
steps.instruction: Visible fiducials (e.g., "X" marks) are previously placed onto MTP Slide Adapter to use as teaching points to register the optical and MS images.

steps.title: For immediate imaging
steps.instruction: For immediate imaging: Place ITO slides in the vacuum desiccator at Room temperature for drying 00:20:00

steps.title: Turn on TM-Sprayer unit
steps.instruction: Turn on TM-Sprayer unit. Set valve to LOAD position. Tissue MALDI TM-Sprayer HTX Technologies, LLC

steps.title: Launch TM-Sprayer Software IMPORTANT
steps.instruction: Launch TM-Sprayer Software IMPORTANT: Check that exhaust fan is operational. Do not start solvent pump if proper active venting is not functioning.

steps.title: Start solvent pump at 0
steps.instruction: Start solvent pump at 0.100 mL/min. Backpressure should be normal. ~ 500 psi (3.4 MPa)

steps.title: Start compressed air flow to TM-Sprayer
steps.instruction: Start compressed air flow to TM-Sprayer. Set at 10 psi (70 kPA).

steps.title: Adjust operating temperature on the sprayer device per solvent mixture
steps.instruction: Adjust operating temperature on the sprayer device per solvent mixture. Follow safety instructions. SOLVENT MIXTURE SHOULD CONTAIN 30% WATER MINIMUM.

steps.title: NOTE
steps.instruction: NOTE: These are, for now, the optimal condition for matric spraying based on METASPACE output and spatial resolution achieved, and we will continue to optimize both matrix applications.

steps.title: With valve in LOAD position, use...
steps.instruction: With valve in LOAD position, use a syringe to fill loop with matrix. 20% overfill is recommended (e.g. Use 6 mL syringe to fill 5 mL loop)

steps.title: Bring ITO glass slide with affixed sample(s) from dessicator
steps.instruction: Bring ITO glass slide with affixed sample(s) from dessicator. Conductive ITO Coating Glass Slides for MALDI Imaging Bruker Catalog #8237001

steps.title: Check that the flow rate of...
steps.instruction: Check that the flow rate of the solvent pump and temperature of the spray nozzle are correct and stable.

steps.title: Review sample position and define spraying area in TM-Sprayer software
steps.instruction: Review sample position and define spraying area in TM-Sprayer software.

steps.title: Select Method by clicking on left column
steps.instruction: Select Method by clicking on left column. Current Method field will confirm selection.

steps.title: Press START
steps.instruction: Press START. Option changes to CONTINUE. Follow STATUS tab for prompts.

steps.title: When prompted, switch valve to Spray...
steps.instruction: When prompted, switch valve to Spray and confirm by clicking Continue.

steps.title: Software will automatically delay start to...
steps.instruction: Software will automatically delay start to allow purging of liquid lines.

steps.title: At end of the run the...
steps.instruction: At end of the run the spray nozzle automatically goes to Waste position.

steps.title: Follow end of run prompts
steps.instruction: Follow end of run prompts. Software will keep track of usage and remaining matrix volume.

steps.title: At the end of sample prep...
steps.instruction: At the end of sample prep sequence, swith the valve back to LOAD. IMPORTANT: Keep solvent pump flow on so that clean solvent flows to nozzle and prevents matrix residues from crystallizing and clogging the capillary and spray nozzle.

steps.title: Spray at 0
steps.instruction: Spray at 0.200 mL/min for 00:10:00 , then at 0.010 mL/min until ready to resume.

steps.title: Switch valve back to LOAD position
steps.instruction: Switch valve back to LOAD position. Set solvent pump flow rate at 0.500 mL/min.

steps.title: Set Temperature to 30 °C to start cool down
steps.instruction: Set Temperature to 30 °C to start cool down.

steps.title: Fill syringe with 5 mL to...
steps.instruction: Fill syringe with 5 mL to 6 mL of clean solvent and flush loop completely. Repeat 3 times.

steps.title: Toggle valve to wash matrix residue
steps.instruction: Toggle valve to wash matrix residue. Leave valve in LOAD position.

steps.title: Keep airflow and solvent pump flow...
steps.instruction: Keep airflow and solvent pump flow on until temperature is below 50 °C . This is important to prevent clogging.

steps.title: Turn N 2 flow off (droplet...
steps.instruction: Turn N 2 flow off (droplet will form at nozzle tip).

steps.title: Add MilliQ water ( 200 µL...
steps.instruction: Add MilliQ water ( 200 µL to 300 µL ) to the biopsy tube containing the remaining fresh frozen (liquid N 2 ) biopsy sample (i.e. not sectioned) and lyse the remaining biopsy sample using a tissue lyser. Quantify the amount of tissue remaining by weight.

steps.title: Place the sample into a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Place the sample into a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube. 2 mL Sorenson MµlTI™ SafeSeal™ Microcentrifuge Tubes VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #53550 It's been shown these tubes do not leach polymers into the lipid layer from the chloroform.

steps.title: Add cold ( -20 °C )...
steps.instruction: Add cold ( -20 °C ) chloroform:methanol mix (prepared 2:1 v/v) to sample in 4:1 ratio over sample volume and vortex. Methanol (HPLC grade) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) (CAS: 67-56-1) Chloroform (HPLC grade) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) (CAS: 67-66-3) i.e. add 400 μl of the 2:1 chloroform:methanol mixture to 80 μl of sample

steps.title: Vortex for 00:00:05 to 00:00:10
steps.instruction: Vortex for 00:00:05 to 00:00:10

steps.title: Vortex for 00:00:05 to 00:00:10
steps.instruction: Vortex for 00:00:05 to 00:00:10

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample 12000 x g,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample 12000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes, 5-10 min

steps.title: Carefully remove the upper aqueous metabolite...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the upper aqueous metabolite layer until the interphase contracts without disturbing the protein disk, and discard.

steps.title: Carefully puncture the protein interphase with...
steps.instruction: Carefully puncture the protein interphase with a pipette tip, remove the organic lipid phase from the bottom of the tube into a conical bottom Waters autosampler vial. Be sure to gently push out any protein or upper methanol phase that might have entered the pipette tip.

steps.title: The organic layer (containing lipids) is...
steps.instruction: The organic layer (containing lipids) is placed into the speed vac to dry.
